summary.intent: Fix GitHub issue #508 where MiniMax provider was incorrectly sending system messages inline in the messages array, causing API error 2013.
summary.outcome: Restored `requires_top_level_system_prompt()` to return true for MiniMax, updated 2 existing tests, added a regression test, and all 240 provider tests pass.

repo: MiniMax's API at api.minimax.io/v1 does not accept role:system entries in the messages array — system messages must be sent as a top-level body['system'] field.
Commit 004ac1fa introduced the regression by changing requires_top_level_system_prompt() to always return false.
MiniMax detection uses three signals: model prefix 'MiniMax-', provider name 'minimax' (case-insensitive), or base URL containing 'minimax'.

code.finding: requires_top_level_system_prompt() must return true for MiniMax to extract system messages into body['system'] instead of leaving them in the messages array.
Test minimax_serialization_extracts_system_messages verifies system messages are removed from array and concatenated into top-level prompt.
Test minimax_stream_request_uses_top_level_system_prompt verifies SSE stream request body has body['system'] and no system role in messages.

workflow: Mock URL query parameters can cause route matching failures in tests — prefer provider name or model name for detection in unit tests rather than base URL.
Running rustfmt after edits is necessary; the file required formatting fixes before the suite was clean.

friction: Dolt/beads issue tracker was down, blocking the attempt to create a tracking issue before starting the fix.
Initial regression test used base URL detection which failed due to query parameter mismatch in mock routing, requiring a rewrite to use model/provider name detection instead.
Required multiple reads of the same openai.rs file to piece together context across different sections.
open_items: Beads/Dolt issue was never created due to the service being down — needs to be filed when the tracker is back up.
PR was committed and pushed but the transcript ends before confirming the PR was successfully created.
